solutions.solution: (a) Number at the centre is the sum of numbers in the rows and columns individually. ∴ Missing number = 83 - (31 + 26) = 26

(a) In the figure, considering the diagonally opposite numbers, the larger number is 4 times the smaller number. As, 2 × 4 = 8, 3 × 4 = 12, 5 × 4 = 20. Similarly, 6 × 4 = 24. Hence, missing number is 24.
(c) 15 × 2 = 30, 2 × 7 = 14, 7 × 9 = 63 and 9 × 15 = 135. Hence, the missing number is 135.
(d) As, 93 - (27 + 63) = 3, 79 - (38 + 37) = 4. Similarly, 67 - (16 + 42) = 9. Hence, number 9 will come in place of question mark.
(b) Letters' positions are added to get the numbers in rows. As, D + T → 4 + 20 = 24 and N + E → 14 + 5 = 19. Similarly, L + x = 14. ∴ 12 + x = 14 ⇒ x = 14 - 12 = 2. ∴ ? = B

(c) Numbers inside the triangles are products of alphabetical positions of letters existing along the sides of triangles. As, D × C × R → 4 × 3 × 18 = 216 and A × P × F → 1 × 16 × 6 = 96. Similarly, N × E × G → 14 × 5 × 7 = 490. ∴ ? = 490, 1 × 5 = 5, 4 × 2 = 8

(a) Here, each letter is represented by the square of its opposite letters position. As, C → 3 + 24 = 27, ∴ 24² = 576 and M → 13 + 14 = 27, ∴ 14² = 196. Similarly, E → 5 + 22 = 27, ∴ 22² = 484. ∴ ? = 484

(b) Letter positions are added till they get converted into single digit. As, Z → 26 + 1 + 1 + 12 + 2 = 41; 4 + 1 = 5, A → L → B. Similarly, N → T → F → G → 14 + 20 + 6 + 7 = 47; 4 + 7 = 11 and 1 + 1 = 2. ∴ ? = 2

(c) Clearly, the numbers along the sides of triangle are the squares of digits of the number at the centre. As, (1)² = 1, (4)² = 16, (3)² = 9. Similarly, (2)² = 4, (3)² = 9, (6)² = 36. Hence, the missing number is 4.
